topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a knot tightening in my chest. I had meant to send a scathing critique of the project proposal to Sarah, but my finger slipped, landing squarely on the contact labeled 'Mr. Henderson,' the notoriously stiff department head. A cold wave washed over me, followed by a hot flush creeping up my neck. I stared at the message I’d typed—a perfectly worded, cutting remark—and then at the contact name. Panic started to bubble up, quickly escalating into a tight, simmering fury. I snatched my phone, my jaw clenching so hard my teeth ached. I needed to delete it, *now*. The silence in my small office felt deafening, amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

"Oh, for crying out loud!" I muttered under my breath, slamming my hand lightly on the desk. The mistake had been monumental. I’d intended to send a simple reminder to my roommate about splitting the utility bill, but instead, I’d accidentally dispatched a lengthy, highly personal rant to my mother, who was currently on a business trip across the country. My breath hitched. I felt a distinct tightening in my throat, and my shoulders hunched up toward my ears. Every muscle in my body seemed coiled, ready to spring. I reread the text, the harsh words I’d typed echoing in the quiet room. I wanted to scream, to physically shake the device until the error vanished.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow. My knuckles were white where I gripped the phone. I had accidentally forwarded a private, embarrassing anecdote to my boss, a man whose approval I desperately craved. A wave of intense agitation washed over me, making my vision narrow slightly. I paced three steps to the window, the glass cool against my forehead. My breathing became shallow and quick. I could feel the heat rising in my face, a burning sensation that had nothing to do with the temperature of the room. I wanted to stomp around, to find the phone and smash it against the wall. The sheer wrongness of the situation made my hands tremble uncontrollably.
